cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question and the passage again. The question is asking which of the following descriptions about Yves Gomes is true. The options are A, B, C, and D. First, let's recall what the passage said about Yves Gomes. He's an illegal immigrant in the US, now a university student in Maryland. He left India at 14 months old with his parents, who were deported more than a year ago. He wants to continue his education, go to medical school, and doesn't want to be deported to India, a country he doesn't really know. He supports the DREAMAct and hopes it will pass so he can stay and contribute. Now, let's evaluate each option. Option A: He was born in India before his parents immigrated to America illegally. The passage says he was born in the US. Wait, actually, the passage says he's an illegal immigrant, but he grew up in the US. Let me check again. The passage says he's one of the young illegal immigrants who grew up in the US. So he was brought to the US as a baby. His parents were deported more than a year ago. So he was born in India? Wait, the passage says he was born in the US? No, actually, the passage says he was born in India. Let me check the exact wording. The passage says: "He is fighting deportation to India, a country he left with his parents when he was just 14 months old." So he left India at 14 months old, which means he was born in India. His parents took him to the US when he was 14 months old. So he was born in India. Therefore, option A says he was born in India before his parents immigrated to America illegally. Well, the parents did immigrate illegally, and he was born in India. So A is partially correct.
